item.title: First Brands chose BDO for ‘less rigorous’ approach, witness says
item.description: Former executive told investigator examining company’s failure that audit firm had ‘most unsophisticated’ process

item.title: RAC pumps the brakes on £5bn London IPO
item.description: Driving group flotation will probably take place in late 2026 as volatile markets push timelines

item.title: DR Congo creates $100mn ‘Mining Guard’ to protect critical minerals
item.description: Security force aims to drive investment in African nation’s resource sector

item.title: Spirit Airlines’ dismal prospects could soon be everyone’s problem
item.description: Lenders may flinch at a government bailout but the alternative could be worse

item.title: Airbus profits halve after delivery slump
item.description: European aerospace and defence group hit by persistent delays receiving engines for its A320s

item.title: CVC weighs €9bn bid for Italian payments group Nexi
item.description: Deal would carve out digital banking solutions arm to prevent Rome using its veto powers

item.title: British army to restart trials of troubled Ajax armoured vehicle
item.description: Reports of vomiting and hearing loss among soldiers the result of ‘a combination of factors’, investigation finds

item.title: Swiss court dismisses case against daughter of former Uzbek president
item.description: Closure of money-laundering proceedings complicates wider corruption probe involving private bank Lombard Odier

item.title: PR executive tried to ‘get rid’ of documents despite legal warning
item.description: Audio recordings reveal Tom Harper told contractor to destroy material about an investigation into journalists

item.title: China poised to restart exporting jet fuel, diesel and gasoline
item.description: Beijing signals relaxation of export ban imposed at start of Iran conflict

item.title: Brighton to build Europe’s first women’s football stadium
item.description: New £80mn arena represents a major vote of confidence in growth potential of WSL

item.title: Downing Street rules out temporary rent freeze
item.description: Chancellor Rachel Reeves stirred speculation by promising to ‘use every lever’ to help renters hit by Iran war fallout

item.title: Small shareholders keep up pressure on UK bank climate policies
item.description: Lower vote in support of NatWest chair at annual meeting

item.title: Reddit may need to sell its soul for scale
item.description: Losing on aggregate, or backpage of the internet?

item.title: Music groups BMG and Concord to merge in $14bn deal
item.description: Combined company will aim to take on record industry’s three major players

item.title: Spotify shares slump as investors fret about impact of price rises
item.description: Market is watching closely for subscribers’ response to increased charges

item.title: Irish bet on gas from kitchen scraps hits policy snag
item.description: Cork plant set to come online next year may end up benefiting UK’s climate goals

item.title: Argentina’s energy economy booms amid Iran shock
item.description: High oil and gas export revenues could be an unexpected windfall for the South American nation

item.title: BYD profits drop by more than half as Chinese sales slow
item.description: Phasing out of electric vehicle subsidies has driven slump in domestic sales growth following years-long boom

item.title: First ‘autocallable’ ETF launched in Europe
item.description: Coupon-paying fund aimed at retail investors

item.title: Grant Thornton US to buy Australian sister firm as part of global spree
item.description: Private equity-backed accounting group vies with UK business to create international network

item.title: ‘Half-amused, half-ashamed’: the employees facing phone bans at work
item.description: Some companies are asking staff to lock devices in pouches to remove distractions and protect data

item.title: Novartis sales fall as blockbuster patents expire
item.description: Swiss pharma group blames competition from generic medicines for 12% drop in first-quarter profit

item.title: Barclays overcomes £200mn credit hit to boost profits
item.description: Fee income in investment banking business helps drive pre-tax profits 3% higher to £2.8bn in first quarter

item.title: BP profits hit highest since 2023 as Iran war delivers windfall
item.description: Oil major’s quarterly profit beats analysts’ expectations on back of ‘exceptional’ trading performance

item.title: Europe’s pharma industry risks lagging behind US and China, says German drugmaker
item.description: Boehringer Ingelheim says pricing regime hinders access to treatments as group touts liver benefits from weight-loss drug

item.title: Wall Street is getting twitchy on oil prices
item.description: Plus, dollar dreams and reality

item.title: FirstFT: Germany’s Merz warns US ‘being humiliated’ by Iran
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: MPs vote on probe into Starmer, and Orbán allies dwindle

item.title: Two Spirit Airlines creditor groups back Trump bailout plan
item.description: Bondholder group remains divided over $500mn on offer from White House that would rescue bankrupt budget carrier

item.title: World’s biggest battery maker to raise $5bn in ‘opportunistic’ share placement
item.description: China’s CATL rides surge in foreign investor interest in renewable energy stocks since outbreak of war in Iran
